title: Does Rei dream
wikitext:
[]: Does Rei dream?             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Jul 25, 2010 2:40 am I've a bad omen about making this thread but I'm going to try it anyway. Something that has always give me doubts is the scene in episode 19 when Asuka ask Rei if she doesn't dream. In that scene there's not a reply from Rei yet it's commonly believed that this should mean that she doesn't dream but after all the lack of a reply doesn't necessarily mean that she doesn't dream. Something that gave me even more doubts (as usual) is the (in)famous NGE2 game. While playing with Rei I started noticing that she had (as many other characters) several event while sleeping, but I refrained from considering them "dreams" considering their nature. But then I arrived at this point: [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LWNViEGeFUc[/url] And in this case she expressly say that it was a dream. As usual there's the problem that many guys doesn't want to consider anything outside NGE and its cryptic hints as worth of analysis but I can't really believe that Rei doesn't dream while dogs and cats (and many other animals) can do it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jul 25, 2010 8:17 am It's rather ambiguous, yeah. Possible reasons for that include, off the top of my head: Maybe she simply doesn't want to rise to Asuka's taunts? Rei's dreams are disturbing Lilith-stuff that she'd rather not think about at all, much less discuss with her. Perhaps her daily pill regimen includes a narcoleptic that prevents her from dreaming? She knows he's actually not dreaming because she's aware of her helltrain self interrogating him at the moment

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sun Jul 25, 2010 9:42 am NemZ wrote: It's rather ambiguous, yeah. Possible reasons for that include, off the top of my head: Maybe she simply doesn't want to rise to Asuka's taunts? Rei's dreams are disturbing Lilith-stuff that she'd rather not think about at all, much less discuss with her. Perhaps her daily pill regimen includes a narcoleptic that prevents her from dreaming? She knows he's actually not dreaming because she's aware of her helltrain self interrogating him at the moment

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Jul 25, 2010 10:17 am NemZ wrote: Rei's dreams are disturbing Lilith-stuff that she'd rather not think about at all, much less discuss with her.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Jul 25, 2010 11:55 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jul 25, 2010 2:13 pm May or may not be relevant: Ritsuko wrote: Light and Water, which make up Rei's deep mentality, reflect the image of this place.

[]: Re: Does Rei dream?             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sun Jul 25, 2010 2:24 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Something that gave me even more doubts (as usual) is the (in)famous NGE2 game.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jul 25, 2010 4:49 pm I'm going with the "Rei's dreams are disturbing and she's rather not think of them" theory, if only because she's had multiple mind-trippy monologues and stuff about "the true self underneath" and whatnot and never seems to comment on the experiences as out of place or unusual.

[]: Re: Does Rei dream?             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jul 26, 2010 4:09 am Lucretius wrote: I hear there was a TV show based on it, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jul 26, 2010 9:53 pm Ep. 14 certainly seems to suggest she DAYdreams, and real dreaming isn't all that different...

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Jul 27, 2010 7:52 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Ep. 14 certainly seems to suggest she DAYdreams, and real dreaming isn't all that different...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Jul 27, 2010 2:41 pm Rei has moments of daydreaming without spirit-contact fuckery all the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jul 27, 2010 10:09 pm Azathoth wrote: Tripping balls due to contact with Yui is quite different from dreaming, unless I'm forgetting which episode you're talking about.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jul 27, 2010 10:17 pm Rei has a mind and is capable of free thought, therefore she must be able to dream while she sleeps, unless of course any medication she takes suppresses dreams.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Jul 28, 2010 3:31 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Ep. 14 is the one with her famous monologue. It doesn't strike me that Yui would be the sole cause of her tripping balls there. Why would Yui be telling her she's a girl that doesn't bleed?

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Wed Jul 28, 2010 5:50 pm If Rei does in fact dream, then her exchange with Asuka has little point. NGE is not a shaky-cam documentary, folks.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jul 28, 2010 6:06 pm Lucretius wrote: If Rei does in fact dream, then her exchange with Asuka has little point. NGE is not a shaky-cam documentary, folks.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Jul 28, 2010 6:11 pm ...

[]: Does Rei dream?             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Jul 25, 2010 2:40 am I've a bad omen about making this thread but I'm going to try it anyway. Something that has always give me doubts is the scene in episode 19 when Asuka ask Rei if she doesn't dream. In that scene there's not a reply from Rei yet it's commonly believed that this should mean that she doesn't dream but after all the lack of a reply doesn't necessarily mean that she doesn't dream. Something that gave me even more doubts (as usual) is the (in)famous NGE2 game. While playing with Rei I started noticing that she had (as many other characters) several event while sleeping, but I refrained from considering them "dreams" considering their nature. But then I arrived at this point: [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LWNViEGeFUc[/url] And in this case she expressly say that it was a dream. As usual there's the problem that many guys doesn't want to consider anything outside NGE and its cryptic hints as worth of analysis but I can't really believe that Rei doesn't dream while dogs and cats (and many other animals) can do it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jul 25, 2010 8:17 am It's rather ambiguous, yeah. Possible reasons for that include, off the top of my head: Maybe she simply doesn't want to rise to Asuka's taunts?
